pokemon:palossand.name: Palossand
pokemon:palossand.summary: Buried beneath the castle are masses of dried-up bones from those whose vitality it has drained.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: Buried beneath the castle are masses of dried-up bones from those whose vitality it has drained.
This Pokémon lives on beaches, but it hates water. Palossand can’t maintain its castle-like shape if it gets drenched by a heavy rain.
Possessed people controlled by this Pokémon transformed its sand mound into a castle. As it evolved, its power to curse grew ever stronger.
Palossand is known as the Beach Nightmare. It pulls its prey down into the sand by controlling the sand itself, and then it sucks out their souls.
Once it has whipped up a sandstorm to halt its opponents in their tracks, this terrifying Pokémon snatches away their vitality.
Each of its grains of sand has its own will. Palossand eats small Pokémon and siphons away their vital essence while they’re still alive.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: Buried beneath the castle are masses of dried-up bones from those whose vitality it has drained.

pokemon:sandygast.name: Sandygast
pokemon:sandygast.summary: It takes control of anyone who puts a hand in its mouth. And so it adds to the accumulation of its sand-mound body.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: It takes control of anyone who puts a hand in its mouth. And so it adds to the accumulation of its sand-mound body.
Sandygast mainly inhabits beaches. It takes control of anyone who puts their hand into its mouth, forcing them to make its body bigger.
Born from a sand mound playfully built by a child, this Pokémon embodies the grudges of the departed.
Grudges of the dead have possessed a mound of sand and become a Pokémon. Sandygast is fond of the shovel on its head.
If you build sand mounds when you’re playing, destroy them before you go home, or they may get possessed and become Sandygast.
It likes the shovel on its head, so Sandygast will get serious and fight any children who come to take it back.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: It takes control of anyone who puts a hand in its mouth. And so it adds to the accumulation of its sand-mound body.
